_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Ranger Suarez hasn't allowed a run in 19 innings heading into Tuesday
items.snark: Suarez could pitch 27 scoreless and we'd still lose 1-0 on an error in the 9th.

items.headline: Gray shuts down Royals in 3-1 win, but Sox offense still anemic
items.snark: Sonny Gray could throw a perfect game and we'd still only score on a passed ball in the 8th.

items.headline: This part of the plan has worked: Gray and Contreras steals paying off
items.snark: Gray and Contreras are legit, which makes it even more painful that we're wasting them.

items.headline: Red Sox offense: If they were league average, they'd be 32-14
items.snark: 32 and 14 with a league average bat? Fire everyone and then fire them again.

items.headline: Roman Anthony setback: Still sore swinging bat, shut down until Thursday
items.snark: Our best hitting prospect can't swing a bat, which perfectly sums up this season.

items.headline: Teams keeping tabs on former Sox pitcher Dustin May ahead of deadline
items.snark: May was terrible for us last year and now other teams want him. Sounds about right.

items.headline: Ceddanne Rafaela out Monday with tight hamstring, day-to-day
items.snark: The one guy hitting gets a hammy tweak because this franchise is cursed.

items.headline: Brayan Bello gets shelled again, gives up 7 runs to Braves in 5 innings
items.snark: Bello without an opener is like bringing a knife to a bazooka fight.

items.headline: Patriots schedule breakdown: 5 types of games from layups to bloodbaths
items.snark: Sports Hub sorting the schedule like Tinder dates: swipe right, swipe left, pray for mercy.

items.headline: Patriots headed to Germany Week 10 for international game vs Lions
items.snark: Munich trip means 9:30 AM kickoff and prayers our plane doesn't fall apart mid-flight.

items.headline: Aaron Rodgers back on Patriots schedule after re-signing with Steelers
items.snark: Rodgers in Week 2 at Gillette, so expect five turnovers and a media circus all week.

items.headline: Patriots scouting report: Dre'Mont Jones brings power to the edge
items.snark: Jones on a three-year, 36 million deal better bring power because our D-line needs a pulse.
